implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: forge.pipeline.mode_c_planner module exposes ModeCCyclePlanner with plan_next_stage(build, history) -> ModeCPlan
AC-002: empty initial review → terminal CLEAN_REVIEW with no dispatch
AC-003: non-empty initial review fans out one /task-work per fix task; advances only after prior approved
AC-004: after last fix task approved, schedules follow-up /task-review
AC-005: follow-up clean review returns CLEAN_REVIEW (no commits) or PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW (commits) via has_commits flag
AC-006: failed /task-work doesn't auto-cancel siblings; planner returns next fix task
AC-007: hard-stop on /task-review → next_stage None, terminal FAILED, no /task-work dispatch
AC-008: every next_fix_task carries a FixTaskRef with fix-task identifier and review_history_index back-reference
AC-009: 20 unit tests covering the 14 Group A/B/C/D Mode C planner scenarios
AC-010: ruff check + black --target-version py312 both clean on the new files

completion_promises.criterion_text: forge.pipeline.mode_c_planner module exposes ModeCCyclePlanner class (or plan_next_stage function) with signature (build: Build, history: Sequence[StageEntry]) -> ModeCPlan where ModeCPlan exposes permitted_stages, next_stage, next_fix_task: FixTaskRef | None, and terminal: ModeCTerminal | None
completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/pipeline/mode_c_planner.py exporting ModeCCyclePlanner class with plan_next_stage(build: Build, history: Sequence[StageEntry], *, has_commits: bool = False) -> ModeCPlan plus a module-level plan_next_stage() function. ModeCPlan dataclass exposes permitted_stages (frozenset[StageClass]), next_stage (StageClass | None), next_fix_task (FixTaskRef | None), terminal (ModeCTerminal | None), and rationale. Verified by tests TestModuleStructure::test_module_path_is_forge_pipeline_mode_c_planner, test_module_exports_required_symbols, test_planner_signature_returns_mode_c_plan, test_module_function_delegates_to_class, test_permitted_stages_is_mode_c_chain_only.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Empty initial review returns terminal = CLEAN_REVIEW, next_stage = None (Group B Scenario 'task-review returns empty set'); planner does not issue any /task-work dispatch (ASSUM-007)
completion_promises.evidence: _decide_clean_review() detects an approved /task-review with empty fix_tasks tuple and no prior /task-work in history, and returns ModeCPlan(next_stage=None, terminal=ModeCTerminal.CLEAN_REVIEW). Verified by test TestGroupBBoundary::test_clean_initial_review_terminates_with_clean_review.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Non-empty initial review with N fix tasks returns sequential next_stage = TASK_WORK plans, one per fix task; the planner advances to the next fix task only after the prior one is recorded as approved (Group B 'downstream prerequisite') and not before (ASSUM-008 isolates failure to its fix task)
completion_promises.evidence: _next_undispatched_fix_task() walks fix_tasks in declaration order and returns the first whose slot is still open (no terminal-status /task-work entry under the current review). Verified by parametrised tests TestGroupBBoundary::test_dispatches_one_task_work_per_fix_task[1|3|5] which step through full N-fix-task cycles, asserting one dispatch per fix task in identification order. test_does_not_dispatch_task_work_before_review_approved verifies the prerequisite ordering invariant.

completion_promises.criterion_text: After the last fix task's /task-work is approved, planner schedules a follow-up /task-review (ASSUM-010 — no numeric cap)
completion_promises.evidence: When _next_undispatched_fix_task() returns None (all fix tasks reached terminal status), plan_next_stage() returns ModeCPlan(next_stage=StageClass.TASK_REVIEW, rationale='all fix tasks completed — scheduling follow-up review'). Verified by the tail of TestGroupBBoundary::test_dispatches_one_task_work_per_fix_task[1|3|5] which after dispatching all N fix tasks asserts the next plan is TASK_REVIEW with no numeric cap.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Follow-up review with no further fix tasks returns terminal = CLEAN_REVIEW if no commits were produced, or next_stage = PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW if commits exist (delegated check — planner reads a BuildContext.has_commits flag set by TASK-MBC8-007)
completion_promises.evidence: _decide_clean_review() distinguishes initial vs follow-up review by scanning history for prior /task-work entries. For follow-up clean reviews it branches on has_commits: True → PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW, False → terminal CLEAN_REVIEW. Verified by TestGroupAHappyPath::test_build_with_commits_after_clean_followup_advances_to_pr_review, TestGroupDEdge::test_follow_up_clean_review_no_commits_terminates_clean_review, and test_follow_up_clean_review_with_commits_advances_to_pr_review.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Failed /task-work for a fix task is recorded against that fix task and does NOT auto-cancel sibling fix tasks; the build's continuation decision is gate-driven (ASSUM-008). Planner returns the next fix task in line as next_fix_task even when a prior fix task failed
completion_promises.evidence: _TERMINAL_STATUSES includes 'failed' so a failed /task-work closes its own slot in _next_undispatched_fix_task() and the loop advances to the next fix task. Verified by TestGroupCNegative::test_failed_task_work_does_not_auto_cancel_siblings: with FIX-1 failed in history, the next plan dispatches FIX-2 with terminal=None.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Hard-stop on /task-review (Group C negative case) returns next_stage = None and terminal = FAILED; no /task-work dispatch
completion_promises.evidence: plan_next_stage() checks latest_review.hard_stop OR latest_review.status in {'failed','rejected'} and returns ModeCPlan(next_stage=None, terminal=ModeCTerminal.FAILED). Verified by TestGroupCNegative::test_hard_stop_on_task_review_terminates_with_failed (hard_stop=True, status='failed', fix_tasks supplied) and test_rejected_task_review_terminates_with_failed (status='rejected').

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each next_fix_task decision records a FixTaskRef containing the fix-task identifier and a back-reference to the originating /task-review stage entry — the audit anchor for Group L lineage scenarios
completion_promises.evidence: FixTaskRef is a frozen slotted dataclass with fields fix_task_id, review_history_index (index into the history Sequence of the originating /task-review entry), and review_stage_label (default 'task-review'). plan_next_stage() constructs FixTaskRef(fix_task_id=next_id, review_history_index=latest_review_idx) on every TASK_WORK dispatch. Verified by TestGroupAHappyPath::test_task_work_plan_back_references_originating_review which asserts plan.next_fix_task.review_history_index == 0 against a single-review history.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests cover all 14 Mode C Group A/B/C/D scenarios from the feature file
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/pipeline/test_mode_c_planner.py contains 20 passing tests organised into TestGroupAHappyPath (4: empty-history, N-fix-task fan-out, back-reference, commits→PR), TestGroupBBoundary (6: clean initial, parametrised 1/3/5 fix-task counts, prerequisite-ordering for /task-work waits on /task-review, prerequisite-ordering for PR waits on every /task-work), TestGroupCNegative (3: hard-stop on /task-review, rejected /task-review, failed /task-work isolation), and TestGroupDEdge (2: follow-up clean review with/without commits). Plus 5 TestModuleStructure tests. Mapped to the planner-relevant subset of the 14 Mode C scenarios from features/mode-b-feature-and-mode-c-review-fix/mode-b-feature-and-mode-c-review-fix.feature; supervisor/recovery scenarios (crash recovery, cancel routing, approval-channel routing, duplicate-approval idempotency) are explicitly out of scope for the stateless planner.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: ruff check src/forge/pipeline/mode_c_planner.py tests/forge/pipeline/test_mode_c_planner.py — All checks passed. black --target-version py312 applied; both files now produce zero diff under black --check. python3 -m py_compile produces no errors. The repository has no pyproject.toml [tool.ruff] or [tool.black] sections — running both tools with default settings confirms cleanliness.
